task_updates.executor_notes: Authored Supabase local config and three Supabase migrations: 001_core mirrors the SQLite core schema with JSONB, timestamptz defaults, BOOLEAN flags, matching checks/indexes, and row-based epic_locks; 002_images adds the images table with required source check and indexes including the partial unique active reference key; 003_external_requests_body adds nullable JSONB request_body. Added SQLite mirror migrations for images and nullable TEXT request_body, registered request_body in _JSON_COLUMNS, and added schema_migrations tracking so SQLiteStore.apply_migrations picks up 002/003 once and remains safe on repeated store initialization. Verification: fresh SQLiteStore showed images table, request_body column, image indexes, and applied migration records for 001/002/003; second initialization on the same DB succeeded. `python -m pytest tests/test_sqlite_store.py -q --tb=no --no-header` passed, and the full `python -m pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` suite passed with 23 tests. Source-tree leaked-prefix grep returned zero matches.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. Supabase 001_core, 002_images, and 003_external_requests_body exist with JSONB/timestamptz/BOOLEAN Postgres schema and required indexes; SQLite mirrors for 002 and 003 exist; request_body is registered in _JSON_COLUMNS; and SQLiteStore.apply_migrations picked up 001/002/003 on a fresh DB and skipped them safely on a second initialization.
